stories.title: Israel’s Vance problem is bigger than JD Vance
stories.summary: When American and Israeli warplanes struck Iran on Feb. 28, Israeli officials let themselves believe the alliance was entering a golden age. Four months later, they are bracing for a future where Israel stands more alone than ever. The vice president of the United States set the stage last week, telling Israel it has almost no friends left in the world, and that it should think hard before turning on the one it has. But the problem for Israel is much bigger than JD Vance, according to seven peop

stories.title: Trump administration asks high court to OK its unprecedented immigration detention policy
stories.summary: The Trump administration is  asking the Supreme Court  to bless its massive expansion of ICE detention — a policy that federal district courts have  overwhelmingly rejected . Solicitor General John Sauer is urging the justices to resolve whether people residing in the U.S. without incident for years — sometimes decades — may be subject to mandatory ICE detention while their deportation proceedings play out. It’s a fight that could determine the fate of millions of people, and it c

stories.title: Trump administration partially lifts Anthropic’s AI export ban
stories.summary: The Trump administration on Friday partially rescinded its export ban on Anthropic’s most advanced artificial intelligence model — deescalating a confrontation that has caused confusion across the American AI industry . The release clears the way for a select group of more than 100 companies and agencies to gain access to the Mythos 5 model, two weeks after the administration imposed restrictions amid fears that the software could be used to launch cyberattacks. But a second advanced Anthropic m

stories.title: Europe heatwave: drought fears in Italy as records tumble around Europe – as it happened
stories.summary: Temperature records broken in Germany, Denmark and Slovakia as Europe remains gripped by heatwave After decades of climate warnings, why is Europe so unprepared for rising heat? Seawater is seeping into Italy’s longest river as the waterway starts to run dry in the heatwave, hitting a farming heartland that produces the milk for Parmesan cheese. The Po River has never fallen this low so early in the year, raising fears of a devastating drought in July in this corner of northern Italy. Continue r

stories.title: Hungary holds first post-Orban Budapest Pride march
stories.summary: Tens of thousands packed Budapest's streets despite scorching heat. Former right-wing leader Viktor Orban tried to ban the march last year as part of a wider LGBTQ+ crackdown.

stories.title: Heatwave breaks records in Germany, Denmark and Czech Republic
stories.summary: An estimated 150 million people are now experiencing temperatures of over 35C across Europe.

stories.title: All quiet on the eastern flank? Nato leaders fear they can no longer rely on US help if Russia attacks
stories.summary: Trump administration’s rhetoric has created so much uncertainty that Poland and Baltic states have fresh doubts as alliance prepares to meet next month A nightmare scenario has been playing on eastern European minds with increasing intensity since Donald Trump returned to the White House: what if Russia attacks and the US does not join the fight? On the rare occasions the question is posed out loud, nobody much likes the answer. In mid-May, at a gathering in Tallinn, the US undersecretary of sta

stories.title: Úgy sikerült elolvasniuk kutatóknak egy kétezer éve megégett papirusztekercset, hogy igazából nem is nyitották ki
stories.summary: Áttörést jelenthet a mesterséges intelligenciára és fejlett képalkotó eljárásokra épült technika.

stories.title: Kapitány István: Magyarország 22 uniós tagállammal együtt bővíti majd az energiatároló kapacitásait
stories.summary: A tárolás kérdése kulcsfontosságú a megújuló energia hatékonyabb felhasználásában.

stories.title: Képeken a hatalmas hőségben vonuló Pride
stories.summary: Az extrém időjárás ellenére sokan mentek ki az idei Pride-ra is, ahol Karácsony Gergely arról beszélt, hogy ő szeretne lenni az, aki összeadja majd az első azonos nemű párt Budapesten, de üzent az új kormánynak is. Volt néhány tüntető is az útvonal mentén, Budaházy Edda kereszttel a kezében próbálta megállítani a menetet.

stories.title: "I want to officiate the first same-sex wedding in Budapest" – Karácsony at Budapest Pride
stories.summary: The mayor of Budapest spoke before the tens of thousands gathered at the first Budapest Pride since Orbán was ousted from office.

stories.title: Három embert állított elő a rendőrség a Budapest Pride napján
stories.summary: A felvonulás mellett nyolc további rendezvényt jelentettek be, de mind békésen lezajlott.

stories.title: Kereszttel sem tudták eltéríteni a Pride-ot, Karácsony akarja összeadni az első melegpárt
stories.summary: Vajon az új kormány hogyan fogja kezelni az LMBTQ emberek jogait? Ez a kérdés foglalkoztatta leginkább a 31. Pride résztvevőit. Budaházy Edda egy kereszttel akarta megállítani a menetet, fokhagymát nem láttunk nála. Összefoglaló a Pride felvonulásról.

stories.title: Négy perc után kifütyülték a nézők Horvátország és Ghána alibifociját, de végül nem lett döntetlen a meccs
stories.summary: Mindkét válogatott bejutott a világbajnokság kieséses szakaszába.

stories.title: 'Operation Purgatory': Magyar moves to demolish Orban system
stories.summary: The new Hungarian government is moving swiftly to dismantle the system of former PM Viktor Orban. The focus is on fighting corruption, reforming the media and protecting democracy.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Heatwave expected to bring Germany and Poland to 40C
stories.summary: Austrian Grand Prix declares heat hazard as dramatic rise in temperatures forecast in central and northern Europe Temperatures are forecast to rise dramatically in parts of central and northern Europe this weekend as the intense heatwave continues . In Germany and Poland, highs up to or exceeding 40C (104F) are expected on both Saturday and Sunday, days after swathes of France experienced similar extreme temperatures. The Austrian Grand Prix, taking place this weekend in Spielberg, has declared

stories.title: Vitézy a kínai nagykövetnek: Hivatalosan is kezdeményeztük a Budapest-Belgrád beruházással kapcsolatos titkosítás feloldását
stories.summary: A közlekedésügyi miniszter azt írta: a cél, hogy a lehető leghamarabb elindulhasson a személyszállítás a vonalon.

stories.title: Megint szivárványos zászlókat hajigáltak a Dunába, ennek is rendőrségi eljárás lett a vége
stories.summary: Tovább robog a zászlógate, ezúttal négy fiatalt fogtak el péntek éjjel a rendőrök, rongálás miatt indult ellenük eljárás.

stories.title: Karácsony Gergely a Pride-on: Én akarok lenni az, aki összeadja majd az első azonos nemű párt Budapesten
stories.summary: Nem volt akkora tömeg a hatalmas hőségben megrendezett Pride-on, mint tavaly, amikor a Fidesz be akarta tiltani a felvonulást, de így is sokan mentek ki az eseményre. Karácsony Gergely arra hívta fel a figyelmet, hogy még mindig hatályban van a Pride-ot betiltó törvény. A szervezők bejelentették, hogy 2027-ben megrendezik az első Pride-ot Szegeden.

stories.title: Délután egykor meg is dőlt a napi maximum hőmérsékleti rekord
stories.summary: Az Időkép szerint Budakalászon 40 fokot mértek. Magyarországot még csak most éri el az a rendkívüli hőség, ami napok óta sújtja Európa nyugati országait.

stories.title: Germany news: Major warship project to be scrapped
stories.summary: The Defense Ministry is dropping a multi-billion-euro project to build F126 frigates, media say. And train services have resumed after a communications glitch overnight halted Germany's rail network . DW has more.

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Ballistic missile attack in Kyiv forces residents into shelters
stories.summary: Latest attack follows civilian deaths on both sides of Russia-Ukraine border on Saturday. What we know on day 1,585 A ballistic missile attack was under way in Kyiv early Sunday , with the city’s mayor warning residents to remain in shelters. “Air defence forces are operating in the capital. Remain in shelters!”, the capital’s mayor Vitali Klitschko said on Telegram. Explosions and several flashes in the sky have been reported. The attack follows civilian deaths on both sides of the ​Russia-Ukra

stories.title: Serbian President Vučić says he will resign within ‘weeks’
stories.summary: Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić on Saturday announced that he will resign in the near future, paving the way for early presidential and parliamentary ‌elections. “These are my last days and weeks as the republic’s president. After that, I will resign,” Vučić told supporters of the ruling Serbian Progressive Party (SNS) at a rally in Belgrade on Saturday, according to media reports . His second and final mandate was due to expire in 2027. His announcement came amid persistent

stories.title: JD Vance claims US holds all the cards in Iran and will win ‘either way’
stories.summary: Vice-president appeared on Bill Maher’s show hours before more military strikes were exchanged in strait of Hormuz JD Vance said on Friday that the US wins “either way” regarding negotiations with Iran , pointing to what he called the destruction of its nuclear program and diminishment as a country. “If we make the final deal, then great,” the US vice-president told HBO’s Bill Maher . “If we don’t make the final deal, their nuclear program is still destroyed. They’re still much weaker as a count

stories.title: Bahrain condemns Iranian tit-for-tat drone attack as ‘flagrant threat’
stories.summary: No immediate reports of damage after Bahrain hit by ‘number of drones’ as ship in strait of Hormuz also targeted Bahrain has said it was attacked by Iran with drones on Saturday, apparently in response to overnight US strikes on Iran . A ship in the strait of Hormuz was also attacked. Bahrain’s foreign ministry said a “number of drones” were launched at the country, though there were no immediate reports of damage. It condemned the attack and described it as a “flagrant threat to the security of

stories.title: Trump says he will nominate Lance Schroyer as next ICE director
stories.summary: Schroyer, whom Trump calls ‘a patriot’, led deportation campaign in Oklahoma under ICE-partnered program Donald ​Trump said on ⁠Saturday ⁠that ​he ‌will ‌nominate ‌Lance Schroyer as ‌the next director of US ​Immigration ⁠and ​Customs ​Enforcement ( ICE ), succeeding David Venturella, who had been performing the duties of the director. Trump said in a Truth Social post that Schroyer “has over 29 YEARS of Law Enforcement experience in Oklahoma — A State where I WON all 77 Counties in 2016, 2020, a

stories.title: US says it struck targets in Iran over ‘continued aggression’ against shipping
stories.summary: Strikes come after retaliatory attacks on Friday following a drone strike on cargo ship in strait of Hormuz The US military has launched further strikes on multiple targets in Iran , the US Central Command (Centcom) confirmed, a day after it had struck Iran in retaliation for a drone attack on a cargo ship in the strait of Hormuz. Centcom said its strikes on Saturday were in “direct response to continued Iranian aggression against commercial shipping”. The US strikes targeted “Iranian military s

stories.title: Ukrainian missiles strike defense plant deep in Russia, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Ukrainian forces hit an artillery manufacturing plant in Russian Volgograd Friday night, in another show that Ukraine is ramping up its strikes inside Russia. So-called Flamingo missiles struck the “Titan-Barricade” manufacturing site in Volgograd, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy posted on X . A fire broke out at the premises of the plant, he added. “This is a major industrial complex where the enemy manufactures artillery systems and specialized military equipment, inc

stories.title: Tech industry grapples with Trump’s AI about-faces
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump’s abrupt shift toward an aggressive and unpredictable oversight regime for artificial intelligence has some in the industry yearning for some Biden-style regulation. It’s a reversal that few would have predicted at the start of Trump’s second term, after he swept into office on a wave of donations from Silicon Valley billionaires who warned that former President Joe Biden’s  AI safety  policy would crush U.S. innovation. Trump had signaled an intention to le

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes: death toll rises again to more than 1,400
stories.summary: Search for survivors continues with nearly 70,000 people reported unaccounted for by their family members The ⁠death toll ⁠in ⁠the twin ​earthquakes that struck ⁠ Venezuela earlier ⁠this ​week ‌has ‌risen to ‌1,430, according to one of the country’s top politicians Jorge Rodríguez. Another 3,200 ​people were injured ⁠and 3,100 ​left homeless ​by the ​disaster, ​the National Assembly president added, speaking ​on ​state television. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Premier Chris Minns says he wants to restore confidence to beachgoers after series of shark sightings and attacks Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Shark-spotting drones will fly from dawn to dusk throughout the year at 70 beaches in New South Wales under an expanded monitoring program, the state government says. stories.title: Ukraine strikes Russian weapons plant, Moscow fuel hub
stories.summary: The two attacks, in Volgograd and Vladimir regions, are part of President Zelenskyy's strategy to bring the war to the Russian people and force Putin into peace negotiations.

stories.title: Military-age Ukrainian men could lose EU protection
stories.summary: The European Commission has suggested withdrawing protection status for military-age Ukrainian males. A number of EU countries support the move — and so does Kyiv.

stories.title: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
stories.summary: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.

stories.title: Bahrain says it was targeted by Iranian drones after US strikes on Iran
stories.summary: Iranian state media reported that strikes hit a telecommunications mast in the Sirik area of southern Iran.

stories.title: UK maritime agency raises Strait of Hormuz threat level after oil tanker reports being struck
stories.summary: It comes after the US military carried out strikes on Iran, which it said were in response to Iranian drone attacks on shipping in the Strait.

stories.title: US strikes Iran after ship attack
stories.summary: The U.S. conducted a strike against Iran on Friday in response to an attack on a commercial ship passing through the Strait of Hormuz, the most significant test yet of the deal to end hostilities that President Donald Trump signed last week. U.S. Central Command said Friday that American aircraft struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites, along with coastal radar sites. On Thursday,  Iran attacked a Singaporean cargo ship  that was exiting the strait along the Omani coast with a o

stories.title: ‘It’s become a litmus test’: wins for Israel critics shine light on key issue for Democrats
stories.summary: Candidates who believe Israel has committed genocide won big in New York – will it push the party to move their way? Regardless of which party wins control of Congress in November, New York City voters have all but ensured that next year a vocal bloc of new House Democrats will arrive on Capitol Hill, elected, in large part, because they believe Israel has committed a genocide in Gaza. That is an upshot of the primary elections held Tuesday in New York, where voters ousted two incumbent House De

stories.title: GB News pundit charged with fraud during time as Labour adviser
stories.summary: Exclusive: Matthew Torbitt faces two counts of fraud by false representation in relation to travel and expense claims A prominent political commentator has been charged with fraud in relation to his time as a Labour adviser, the Guardian can reveal. Matthew Torbitt, a regular guest pundit on GB News, faces two counts of fraud by false representation in relation to his travel and expenses claims while working in parliament. Continue reading...

stories.title: Lebanon-Israel deal may stop war crime victims seeking justice, experts say
stories.summary: Agreement could also end hopes of ICC jurisdiction in Lebanon, which advocates pushed for to prosecute Israel A new agreement between Lebanon and Israel could block victims of Israeli war crimes in Lebanon from pursuing accountability and hinder future efforts to give the international criminal court jurisdiction in the country, legal experts have said. Lebanon and Israel signed a 14-point framework agreement in Washington on Friday designed to work towards an end to fighting between Israel and

stories.title: Germany and Italy swelter in heatwave as records tumble across Europe
stories.summary: Denmark experiences highest temperature on record on Saturday as weather system spreads eastward Europe heatwave – latest updates Germany ⁠and Italy endured sweltering conditions on Saturday as a heatwave linked to dozens of deaths in western Europe spread eastwards, after temperatures broke records above 40C (104F). Denmark registered its highest temperature on record on Saturday, according to the Danish meteorological institute. “With 36.6C north of Odense, we have the warmest day ever since m

stories.title: Utah’s Cottonwood fire spreads overnight to cover 92,000 acres
stories.summary: Firefighters are battling the blaze in Fishlake national park that was stoked by strong winds and low humidity Hundreds of firefighters in Utah have struggled to suppress a wildfire that scorched an additional 20,000 acres (8,000 hectares) as of Saturday, as low humidity and strong winds accelerated the fire spread, according to state officials. The Cottonwood fire erupted on Monday in the Fishlake national forest, located in central Utah. The blaze intensified overnight, growing from about 70,0

stories.title: Pro-One Nation Facebook groups appear to be run by foreign ‘meme factories’ that monetise content
stories.summary: Exclusive: Guardian analysis suggests several groups with thousands of members run by what expert calls ‘engagement farm’ operations in south-east Asia Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Some of the largest One Nation supporter groups on Facebook appear to be run from overseas by foreign digital creators who monetise content. stories.title: Man arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after car hits pedestrians in London
stories.summary: Suspect held after five people injured, including three who were taken to hospital, in Ealing on Saturday afternoon A man has been arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after a vehicle hit multiple pedestrians on a busy London road before driving away, police have said. Five people were injured, including three who were later taken to hospital, during the incident in Ealing Broadway, Ealing, west London, at about 2.30pm on Saturday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Four people have died from flash floods in Kentucky, governor says
stories.summary: Floods caused by thunderstorms that have dumped inches of rain on Kentucky and Indiana, with more possible Andy Beshear, the Kentucky governor, says four people have died as a result of flash floods from thunderstorms that have brought as much as 7in (18cm) of rain to the state. Beshear confirmed the four deaths in a social media post, noting three people were from Madison county and one was from Jackson county. “Please join Britainy and me as we pray for their families during this difficult tim

stories.summary: Follow the day’s updates live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Big tech not cooperating ‘as much as we’d like them to’: Watt Federal cabinet minister Murray Watt , says the government knows it’s needed to take more action to ensure social media giants are complying with a ban for under 16s that came into effect in December. stories.title: New Caledonia polls open in first local vote in the French territory since 2019
stories.summary: Election will determine balance of power in New Caledonia before fresh negotiations with France on the territory’s status Polls opened in the French overseas territory of New Caledonia on Sunday for the archipelago’s first provincial elections since 2019, after the vote was delayed as talks stalled over its political future. The election, initially planned for 2024, will determine the balance of power in New Caledonia ahead of fresh negotiations with France on the territory’s status, with indepe

stories.title: Australian man arrested in Thailand after 17-year-old’s body found in suitcase
stories.summary: The 46-year-old was stopped at about 9.30pm on Friday while preparing to board a Jetstar flight to Perth, according to police and local media Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An Australian man has been arrested at a Thai airport in connection with the alleged murder of a 17-year-old girl whose naked body was found in a suitcase, according to local police. The man , 46, was stopped at about 9.30pm on Friday while preparing to travel on a Jetstar flight to Perth, accord

stories.title: Trump threatens 100% tariff on European countries that impose digital tax
stories.summary: US president says levy would be imposed immediately and supersede pre-existing trade deals with the country Donald Trump has threatened to place a 100% import tariff on any European country that imposes a tax on digital services from US companies. Writing on Truth Social on Friday, the US president said that “numerous European countries” had been discussing putting a digital services tax on American companies and that “some of these countries are close to actually doing this”. Continue reading..

stories.title: Australia to double penalty for social media ban breaches to $99m as tech giants accused of ‘not doing enough’
stories.summary: Prime minister Anthony Albanese says too many children still on platforms but he is ‘heartened’ by world-leading law Social media bans go global: big tech faces a reckoning after Australia’s crackdown Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The federal government will double the penalty for breaches of Australia’s youth social media ban to $99m, arguing tech companies are “not doing enough” to keep children off harmful s

stories.title: Rome celebrates 80 years of Italy's iconic Vespa scooter
stories.summary: Thousands of Vespa riders took to Rome's streets to mark Vespa's 80th birthday. The wasp-waisted scooter became a symbol of Italy's rebirth after World War II.

stories.title: Germany news: Heat record broken for second consecutive day
stories.summary: As Europe's heatwave rages, Germany breaks all-time temperature record for the second day in a row. Elsewhere, politicians bicker over reforms and industry blames train operator DB for billions in losses.

stories.title: Why is the US targeting Germany's drug industry?
stories.summary: A new trade probe is reigniting a long-running clash over who should pay for medical innovation. Washington argues Germans benefit from lower prices while US patients shoulder a disproportionate share of the costs.

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Nearly 7 million may be impacted — UN
stories.summary: The projection includes up to two million people in the capital, Caracas. Rescue teams are racing against time to save survivors, three days after the twin quakes that killed 1,430 people. DW has more.

stories.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
stories.summary: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

stories.title: Europe send search and rescue personnel to Venezuela in response to massive quakes
stories.summary: The European Union has begun sending aid to Venezuela through its Civil Protection Mechanism in response to Wednesday's earthquakes of 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude, which have left more than 50,000 people missing, according to the UN.

stories.title: Israel's Netanyahu welcomes US-brokered Lebanon agreement as far right condemns it
stories.summary: Israel's National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir rejected the U.S.-brokered agreement, saying Lebanon could not be trusted to disarm Hezbollah..

stories.title: 'Every person saved is a miracle': Families call to trapped loved ones in region devastated by Venezuela quakes
stories.summary: Families keep vigil at buildings where they fear their loved ones are trapped, but face an impossible task to move heavy debris.

stories.title: Panic attacks and fractures - Venezuela hospital treats earthquake victims
stories.summary: The BBC travelled to a Caracas hospital that is treating people from the worst affected areas.

stories.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
stories.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

stories.title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to 1,430 as searches continue for survivors
stories.summary: More than 520 emergency responders from across the EU have been mobilised to support search and rescue efforts in Venezuela, the European Commission said.

stories.title: The ‘Pride Match’ that wasn’t
stories.summary: SEATTLE — As a lesbian raised in Egypt, Noha Mahgoub could have chosen to dress for what  local organizers branded a “Pride Match”  in colors associated with either her sexual orientation or her ethnic identity. The 43-year-old Democratic legislative aide — one of the top staffers in Washington state government — chose the latter, arriving in a red Egyptian national team jersey, a black hat emblazoned with YALLA and red, white and black tricolor facepaint. “I’ve seen Pride shirts. I’ve

stories.title: Snow and ice on Swiss glaciers melting at alarming rate amid heatwave, expert says
stories.summary: Accumulation on Switzerland’s glaciers from last winter expected to all be gone by Monday amid ‘enormous’ melt rates across Alps Europe heatwave: latest updates Swiss glaciers are set to lose an enormous amount of ice due to the heatwave battering Europe, according to the head of Glacier Monitoring in Switzerland (Glamos). The snow and ice accumulated last winter by Switzerland’s glaciers is expected to have all melted away by Monday, marking the alarming second-earliest arrival on record of the

stories.title: Do you really need to speak German to take a cooling dip? This row in Halle raises all manner of red flags | Fatma Aydemir
stories.summary: A pool manager invoked safety to bar non-German speakers during the heatwave. With the far right soaring, the move is making everyone less safe Humans are vulnerable in water. Beaches have red flags; swimming pools have flashy warning signs to remind us of our vulnerability when we just want to cool down in the midst of a searing heatwave. Pool rules are essential, especially when children are around, or tourists who don’t know about the local safety measures. With pictograms and whistling lifeg

stories.summary: As a host of countries move to rein in social media use by children, could this be technology’s big tobacco moment? Continue reading...

stories.title: Venice protest planned for US ambassador’s superyacht visit
stories.summary: Activists aim to repeat disruption of Jeff Bezos’s wedding when billionaire Tilman Fertitta drops anchor Protesters in Venice are planning to disrupt a visit by the billionaire US ambassador to Italy in his 117-metre superyacht, which they fear he plans to dock in the lagoon city. “We ruined the party for Jeff Bezos’s wedding last year – this year let’s ruin the ambassador’s tour!” said Stella Faye, a 28-year-old researcher and activist, at a meeting of about 40 demonstrators on Thursday. stories.title: Where Copenhagen leads, the food world still follows

During 12 years of living Danishly, with regular trips to the capital, I just … liked most things. Danes specialise in high-quality, organic produce, eaten as close to its natural state as possible. stories.title: ‘A sad inevitability’: after decades of climate warnings, why is Europe so unprepared for rising heat?
stories.summary: Scorching summer of 2003 triggered first efforts to deal with the problem but heatwaves still have devastating impact On Wednesday, Pierre Masselot received a text from his daughter’s nursery – less than 50 miles from the weather station that was the first this week to break the UK June temperature record – asking parents to collect children early because the school buildings were about to get worryingly hot. Similar scenes were repeated across Europe this week as the continent swelters through

stories.title: Children embrace Cornish language as it enjoys ‘remarkable resurgence’
stories.summary: Go Cornish Celebration engages younger generation as council finalises strategy to boost everyday use of Kernewek Seven-year-old Albie, a pupil at Trewirgie infants’ school in Redruth, did not hesitate when asked why he liked learning Kernewek, the Cornish language. “We used to talk this way in the old days,” he said. “And I like speaking now. I enjoy the songs we sing, the Cornish books we read, all the words. It’s fun.” Continue reading...

stories.summary: Passengers report being stuck on grounded planes in sweltering conditions as severe weather causes travel disruption across Europe Europe heatwave: latest updates Thunderstorms have caused severe delays to hundreds of flights at Heathrow and Gatwick airports, leaving passengers stuck on grounded planes for hours in the scorching heat. stories.title: India news: PM Modi embarks on two-day Seychelles visit
stories.summary: India's PM Narendra Modi is set to be the Guest of Honor at the Golden Jubilee of Seychelles' National Day. His visit is also aimed at stregnthening martime, security and economic cooperation. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Balochistan activist's life sentence raises fear of unrest
stories.summary: With the conviction of a leading rights campaigner, Pakistan has drawn criticism from rights groups and sparked fears it could deepen unrest in Balochistan and weaken trust in peaceful activism.

stories.title: Europe faces scorching weekend as heat wave moves east
stories.summary: Europe's heat wave is expected to move east after causing record temperatures in several countries. But Germany still has a couple of torrid days ahead. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Why Germany's Deutsche Bahn will face delays for many years
stories.summary: Delayed, unreliable and forced into long detours due to construction: Germany no longer sets the pace when it comes to rail travel — and this is unlikely to change anytime soon. The company is mired in a deep crisis.

stories.title: US allows partial release of Anthropic's Mythos AI model
stories.summary: Only a small group of American firms will get access to Anthropic's AI Model Mythos 5, however, it is unclear which companies will be selected.

stories.title: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified documents
stories.summary: Bolton faces a prison sentence of up to five years and has agreed to pay $2.25m in fine, prosecutors say.

stories.title: Billionaire Leon Black walks out of Epstein investigation hearing
stories.summary: The House panel then issued subpoenas demanding the private equity tycoon, who co-founded Apollo Global Management, testify on camera and under oath.

stories.title: Belgium through to World Cup knockout stage as Iran forced to wait
stories.summary: Iran now face an anxious wait to see if they'll progress to the round of 32 after their final group game finished 1-1 against Egypt.

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes kill 920 people as international rescue teams arrive
stories.summary: Hundreds are still feared trapped under the rubble, as families desperate for news.

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes: striking satellite images before and after
stories.summary: Venezuela was hit by two earthquakes last Thursday, with the La Guaira area particularly hard hit. Here is a series of before-and-after images showing the scale of the disaster.

stories.title: US strikes Iran after attack on cargo ship
stories.summary: Iran accuses the US of violating their deal and says it struck targets linked to American forces in response.

stories.title: Woman rescued after car plunges into flooded sinkhole
stories.summary: A police officer rescued a woman after her car fell into a sinkhole caused by a burst water main in Virginia, US. Bodycam footage shows bystanders helping steady the officer as he pulled the driver to safety.

stories.title: Newborn baby rescued from Venezuela earthquake rubble
stories.summary: Two powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela within seconds of each other on Wednesday, killing at least 920 people.

stories.title: Anthropic cleared to restore Mythos 5 access to certain US organisations
stories.summary: "We’re restoring access for these organizations quickly, and we’re continuing to work with the government to expand access to Mythos 5 and make Fable 5 available for general use again," the company said on Friday.

stories.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup: Which third-place teams will reach the knockout stage?
stories.summary: The eight best third-place sides are determined first by points and then by goal difference, with the final standings to be decided on Saturday night.

stories.title: Burkina Faso severs diplomatic ties with France
stories.summary: The West African country's junta accused the former colonial power of undermining its national interests.

stories.title: In Caracas, this feels like the hardest moment in Venezuela's modern history
stories.summary: Rescue teams are working ceaselessly to reach those trapped under rubble. But as hope fades, anger is growing.

stories.title: Nagy Ervin: Nem akartam, hogy én legyek a Tisza Eperjes Károlya
stories.summary: Részleteiben újraindultak az NKA-kifizetések, új NFI-pályázat jön, a klubok pályázhatnak majd támogatásért, és hamarosan bejelentenek még egy nevet, aki a filmreformért felel majd – többek közt ezekről is beszélt a Fishing on Orfűn a kultúráért felelős államtitkár.

stories.title: Édesanyja temetése után visszatért a foci-vb-n szereplő francia válogatotthoz Didier Deschamps
stories.summary: A szövetségi kapitány kihagyta a norvégok elleni meccset, de nélküle is simán nyertek a franciák.

stories.title: Verstappen kicsúszása zavart hozott az osztrák időmérőn, Russell kis kavarodás után megtarthatta az első helyét
stories.summary: Úgy tűnt, Russell dupla sárga zászlós szakaszon ment át Verstappen kicsúszása után, de hamar tisztázódott a helyzet.

stories.title: Ukrán rakétatámadás ért egy volgográdi üzemet, Zelenszkij szerint itt gyártják az oroszok szuperfegyvereit
stories.summary: A volgográdi kormányzó szerint a támadásban meghalt egy ember, 11-en megsérültek.

stories.title: Megtalálták a Texasban eltűnt zsiráfot
stories.summary: Az állat nagyjából két hete kóborolt el egy állatparkból, most pár kilométerrel arrébb találtak rá.

stories.title: Az internet domináns cégével befektetőként is nehéz versenyezni
stories.summary: Jelentős részben felvásárlásainak köszönheti jelenlegi pozícióját a Google, de óriási nyereséget könyvelhetett el a tőkebefektetésein is, legutóbb például a SpaceX tőzsdére lépésén.

stories.title: A Ryanair innentől nem kér felárat azért, hogy a gyerekek a szüleik mellé ülhessenek a járataikon
stories.summary: A cég a hatóságok nyomására hozta meg a döntést, nem csak úgy felebaráti szeretetből.

stories.title: Lejár a foci-vb váratlan sztárjának szerződése, új csapatot keres a 40 éves zöld-foki-szigeteki kapus
stories.summary: Vozinha jelenleg az Argentína elleni meccsre készül, a világbajnokság után is folytatná a pályafutását.

stories.title: Átvágták a lakatot, és bevonultak civilek a Fertő tó egykori strandjának területére
stories.summary: Udvaros Dorottyával az élen a közel nyolc éve elzárt vízpartig sétáltak a környezetvédők és a velük tartók.

stories.title: „Ez nem csak egy futballmeccs lesz” – motivációs levelet írt a válogatottnak a Kongói DK elnöke a sorsdöntő vb-meccs előtt
stories.summary: A labdarúgás a Kongói Demokratikus Köztársaságban is nemzeti ügy. Üzbegisztán ellen a továbbjutás lesz a tét.

stories.title: Valami nem stimmel – egészen furcsa dolgokat művel az idei vb-labda
stories.summary: Az Adidas különleges technológiával alkotta meg a Triondát, egy tulajdonsága meglepte a kapusokat. A dolog mögött egy furcsa fizikai jelenség van.

stories.title: Aleksandar Vučić bejelentette, hogy már csak néhány hétig marad köztársasági elnök, utána lemond
stories.summary: Azt is mondta, előrehozott elnökválasztást és parlamenti választást tartanak hamarosan, ő pedig az elnöki posztról távozva is aktív politikai szereplő marad. Szerb lapok azt találgatják, hogy megpróbálhat visszaülni a kormányfői székbe.

stories.title: Kulcscsontsérülést szenvedett a spanyol válogatott szélsője, véget ért számára a világbajnokság
stories.summary: Yeremy Pino egy ütközés után szerencsétlenül ért földet.

stories.title: Csányi Sándor, Világi Oszkár és Váradi József is lemondott egyetemi kuratóriumi tagságáról
stories.summary: Hernádi Zsolt Mol-vezér hasonló lépése után többen is lemondtak kekvás pozíciójukról. Amióta a Tisza Párt bejelentette a kekvák működési modelljének átalakítását, feltűnően nagy a mozgás.

stories.title: 1600 tagú nemzetközi mentőcsapattal keresik a venezuelai földrengések áldozatait
stories.summary: Már több mint 1400-an haltak meg a fővárost és környékét ért katasztrófában.

stories.title: Az amerikai légicsapásra válaszul Irán drónokkal támadta Bahreint
stories.summary: Benjámin Netanjahu közben hatalmas sikerként könyvelte el, hogy az Egyesült Államok és Libanon is elismerte Izrael jogát egy libanoni biztonsági zóna fenntartására.

stories.title: Horvátország–Ghána 2-1
Panama–Anglia 0-2
A potyagólokat kapó uruguayi kapus elnézést kért a csapattársaitól a vb-kiesés után
stories.summary: Uruguay négy gólt kapott a világbajnokságon, abból háromban benne volt a veterán Fernando Muslera.

stories.title: Fehér Enikő: Jön a hidegfront
stories.summary: „Nem vehetek be gyógyszert, ártana a babának. Amikor mérlegeltük, melyikünk hordja ki, nem gondoltunk a migrénemre.” E heti novellánk egy várandósság pillanatképeit villantja fel.

stories.title: Még egy betonsúllyal rögzített napernyőt is az égbe repített a porördög a pesterzsébeti strandon, hárman megsérültek
stories.summary: A jelenség ilyenkor, a nyári melegben a leggyakoribb.

stories.title: Az ausztrál kormány csillagászati összeggel bírságolná a techcégeket, akik nem tiltják ki a 16 éveseket a közösségi médiából
stories.summary: 16 év alatt elvileg tilos a közösségi média használata, de a gyerekek könnyen kijátszák az ellenőrzéseket.

stories.title: Paul McCartney-nak eszében sincs leállni
stories.summary: Az életmű lezárásának is tűnhet McCartney új albuma, de erről szó sincs, a zenész továbbra is remek formában van. Ott voltunk azon a bensőséges londoni eseményen, ahol McCartney a gyerekkoráról és az új dalairól is sztorizott.

stories.title: Egy óra szenvedés, majd öt perc foci – Anglia nehezen, de behúzta a csoportelsőséget
stories.summary: Majdnem megismételte a Ghána elleni teljesítményét Anglia Panama ellen. Kane-ék csak a szünet után tudták feltörni a panamaiak védekezését.

stories.title: Kolumbia–Portugália 0-0
Kongó–Üzbegisztán 3-1
Algéria–Ausztria 0-1
Jordánia–Argentína 0-2
Tracing one delicious snack around the Mediterranean showed me that modern borders are absurd | Federico De Blasi
stories.summary: Migration and cultural exchange have always been the norm between coastal European and African nations. We should celebrate this shared history We are used to mapping the world by continents, dividing the globe into rigid geopolitical blocks. But to understand the complex reality behind each border, we are better off using a different, edible kind of cartography. For most of human existence, the Mediterranean has existed as an intercultural entity in its own right, where peoples and languages fr

stories.title: South Korea to train half a million military personnel to become ‘drone warriors’
stories.summary: All branches of the military will be taught how to use technology that has become a ‘game changer on the battlefield’, says defence minister All of South Korea’s military forces will be trained as drone operators in a sweeping overhaul of its warfare strategy, the defence minister has said. “All soldiers should be able to use drones like a second personal firearm,” Ahn Gyu-back, who heads the defence ministry in Seoul, said on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Outrage as woman jailed for three years after criticising Somali government online
stories.summary: Sentencing of 27-year-old Sadia Moalim Ali condemned by former president and prime ministers as well as rights groups A rickshaw driver in Somalia has been sentenced to three years in prison for comments she made on social media, in a case that has caught the public’s attention and provoked outrage in the country. Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old nursing graduate, was originally charged with insulting government institutions and incitement to commit a crime, but convicted only of the former. Her

stories.title: Spain's military firefighters battle wildfire in Huesca province – video
stories.summary: Firefighters worked to halt a wildfire near Tamarite de Litera in Huesca province, where flames have scorched at least 1,000 hectares (2,470 acres). Firefighters from the military emergency unit worked through Thursday night to contain the blaze, clearing vegetation and using hoses to prevent the fire from spreading. The Aragon regional government declared a level 2 emergency on Thursday. Hundreds of people were evacuated from three municipalities as authorities sought to protect nearby communit

stories.title: US carries out 'additional strikes' on Iran
stories.summary: The US military said "additional strikes" have been carried out on targets in Iran. CENTCOM said the strikes were in response to a drone strike on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz. DW has more.

stories.title: Montenegro police arrest Iranian accused of hacking US universities
stories.summary: The suspect is accused of helping steal data from more than 150 US universities over a hacking campaign dating back to 2013.

stories.title: Watch: South Korea holds first live-fire drill against drone swarms
stories.summary: South Korea's air force carried out its first live-fire exercise against a simulated swarm drone attack. Military forces used Vulcan air-defense guns, a portable laser and shotguns to intercept all 50 drones during the drill.

stories.title: Von der Leyen to visit Azerbaijan and Armenia to build on EU engagement
stories.summary: The European Commission president will visit Baku and Yerevan to build on the EU’s strategic engagement with the South Caucasus and to strengthen energy cooperation, trade and regional connectivity for the EU economies.

stories.title: Ukraine: Zaporizhzhia ablaze after Russian missile and drone attack leaves at least nine hurt
stories.summary: Russian missile and drone strikes set an office building ablaze in central Zaporizhzhia on 26 June, injuring at least nine people and triggering a major emergency response.

stories.title: Venezuela updates earthquake toll: 920 dead, 3,360 injured
stories.summary: National Assembly president Jorge Rodríguez updated the figure on Friday. The UN reports over 50,000 missing. Spain urges its citizens in Venezuela to contact consular emergency services.

stories.title: Small plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building
stories.summary: Police and emergency crews sealed off the area around Beijing's tallest skyscraper after a small plane crash damaged the building. Images from the scene showed a large hole in the upper floors of the 528-metre CITIC Tower, while witnesses reported seeing aircraft debris at the base of the building.

stories.title: Pete Buttigieg briefly separated from children after false police report
stories.summary: Buttigieg says the day he spent separated from his twins was "among the darkest hours of my life".

stories.title: US Supreme Court clears way for mass deportations of Haitians and Syrians
stories.summary: The 6-3 ruling hands Donald Trump a sweeping victory on immigration, stripping legal protections from hundreds of thousands of people the State Department warns would be returning to dangerous countries.

stories.title: Brexit: An Oral History – podcast
stories.summary: This week marked 10 years since the UK chose to leave the EU. In a series of interviews with key players from both sides, Kiran Stacey looks back on the Brexit vote that changed the country forever Read the Guardian’s full article on Brexit here Continue reading...

stories.title: Child malnutrition in Nepal has reached ‘alarming’ levels since aid cuts, survey finds
stories.summary: Fears hard-won gains in reducing child mortality over 20 years are at risk after end of USAID funding for nutrition programmes Child malnutrition in Nepal has reached “alarming” levels, according to the largest ever survey of under-fives in the country. The new figures came just over a year after USAID, the former US flagship agency closed by the Trump administration in 2025, stopped funding work on child nutrition in Nepal. Continue reading...

stories.title: Whereabouts of nearly 300 people with Ebola unknown in DR Congo
stories.summary: Fears over ‘huge community transmission’ as modelling predicts thousands of deaths in DRC by September The whereabouts of almost 300 people who have tested positive for Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is unknown, according to Africa’s top public health official. The humanitarian crisis amid the conflict in the affected areas means more than 1 million people are living in camps to which health workers have no access, Dr Jean Kaseya, director general of the Africa Centers for Disease

stories.title: ‘Everyone is talking about Cape Verde’: World Cup run delights diaspora community in UK
stories.summary: Cape Verdeans in Britain feeling ‘incredibly proud’ after team’s hard-fought draws against Spain and Uruguay World Cup live – latest updates For as long as she can remember, 13-year-old Lauryn struggled to find a map that included Cape Verde. Now, to her great delight, the tiny African island nation is finally centre stage. “Seeing our country shown across the world at the World Cup makes me feel incredibly proud,” Lauryn says. “After the first match, everyone was talking about Cape Verde. Peopl

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes: 589 confirmed dead so far as international rescue teams arrive – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story here China’s president Xi Jinping said Beijing was ready to provide Venezuela with “disaster relief and reconstruction” assistance. Xi sent a message of condolence to Venezuela’s interim president Delcy Rodríguez today, according to Chinese state news agency Xinhua. Continue reading...

stories.title: Survivors tell of ‘brutal and fast’ Venezuela quake as hunt for survivors goes on
stories.summary: Worst incident of its kind in country for more than 125 years leaves many searching for family members – and pleading for international help Venezuela death toll doubles as interim president vows to save ‘as many people as possible’ Nearly all of Ligia Level’s family lived in a trio of apartment blocks along Hotel Avenue, a seafront sweep of palm-specked resorts and high-rise condos along Venezuela’s Caribbean coast. When a powerful “doublet” of earthquakes jolted the region on Wednesday afterno

stories.title: Carney announces contest to revamp uninhabitable Canadian PM residence
stories.summary: Various issues – including a rodent infestation and mould – have left the historic, sprawling Ottawa estate empty 10 Downing Street has two things: mice and a chief mouser . For more than a decade, an officially recognized feline has kept rodent infestation in the British prime minister’s residence to a minimum. Over a similar period, the official residence of Canada’s prime minister has seen an unchecked explosion of rodents. Continue reading...

stories.title: Venezuela quake death toll reaches 920 as interim president vows to save ‘as many as possible’
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez says foreign rescue teams are arriving as anger grows at official response and limited resources Survivors tell of ‘brutal and fast’ Venezuela quake as hunt for survivors goes on Venezuela’s interim president, Delcy Rodríguez, has vowed to fight to save “as many people as possible” as the official death toll from the country’s worst earthquake in more than a century almost doubled, but frustration was growing at the perceived sluggishness of the government’s response. Rodríguez’s

stories.title: Katasztrófa – kiborult az iráni csapatkapitány, a FIFA elnöke nem teljesítette az ígéretét
stories.summary: Mehdi Taremi szerint nincs egyenlő versenyhelyzet, Gianni Infantino hiába ígért segítséget, nem történt semmi.

stories.title: Az ukránok láthatóan jól felépített, stratégiai terv szerint hajtják végre a dróncsapásokat
stories.summary: Zelenszkij 40 napos offenzívát jelentett be, ami lényegében az ukránoknak a Krím és a mélyen Oroszországban lévő területek ellen már eddig is felpörgött dróntámadásainak kiterjesztése. Takács Márk katonai szakértővel elemeztük, mik ennek a céljai, a kockázatai és hogyan reagálhat Putyin.

stories.title: A rendkívüli hőség kockázataira figyelmeztetett Hegedűs Zsolt a hétvégi tömegrendezvények előtt
stories.summary: Az egészségügyi miniszter azt írta, több szabadtéri rendezvény is lesz a hétvégén, ezért mindenkit arra kér, hozzon felelős döntést a részvételről.

stories.title: A szósz, amiért megőrülnek a foci-vb-re látogató turisták
stories.summary: Mindenki ranch szószos flakonokkal indul haza a világbajnokságról. De miért bolondulnak a turisták egy zöldfűszeres-majonézes öntetért?

stories.title: Ürge-Vorsatz Diána: A fák jelentik az egyetlen valódi védelmet az extrém hőhullámok ellen
stories.summary: A klímakutató szerint fontos, hogy ne vágják meg túlságosan a városi fák lombjait, és az öreg fákat ne vágják ki.

stories.title: Takács Péter: Hiba volt a kötelező üzemorvosi vizsgálat eltörlése
stories.summary: Szerinte ezt az ipar lobbizta ki magának a kis- és középvállalkozásokon keresztül, ő viszont ellenezte.

stories.title: Feszült helyzetben találkozott egymással Putyin és az ukránok ultimátumának eleget tevő Lukasenko
stories.summary: Dmitrij Peszkov, a Kreml szóvivője előre közölte, hogy nem lesz sajtótájékoztató és közösen aláírt nyilatkozat sem. De a tárgyalások ma is folytatódnak majd.

stories.title: Szerdán nevezhetik ki a közmédia ideiglenes vezetését
stories.summary: Tarr Zoltán szerint társadalmi és szakmai párbeszédet folytatnak majd arról, milyen közmédiát akarnak az emberek.

stories.title: Megszüntették a Pride megszervezése miatt indított büntetőeljárást Karácsony Gergely ellen
stories.summary: A büntetőügyet még márciusban felfüggesztették, most a bíróság kimondta, nem sértett gyülekezési jogot a főpolgármester.

stories.title: Annyira forrók a sínek, hogy a MÁV sok vonalon 20 kilométer per órával csökkentette a vonatok sebességét
stories.summary: Ezért kisebb késésekre is lehet számítani az érintett vonalakon.

stories.title: Magdeburg Christmas market attacker sentenced to life in prison
stories.summary: A Magdeburg court has sentenced a 51-year-old to life over his 2024 attack on a Christmas market in the city. The trained psychiatrist drove an SUV through a barricade and into a crowd, killing six and injuring hundreds.

stories.title: Here's what southern Europeans know about surviving heat waves
stories.summary: People in countries like Greece, Italy and Spain have long lived with high temperatures. Here's what more northerly countries can learn from them about managing heat waves as they become more common.

stories.title: Fact check: As Europe bakes, should you trust your sunscreen?
stories.summary: As a heatwave sweeps across Europe, claims are resurfacing online questioning whether sunscreen is safe or linked to skin cancer risk.

stories.title: Bayer: Why so many Roundup weedkiller lawsuits?
stories.summary: Bayer was sued for failing to add cancer warnings to its controversial pesticide Roundup when US regulators opposed adding one. DW asks if the latest Supreme Court win will shield the chemical giant from paying billions.

stories.title: Wagner festival reinstates Holocaust memorial event after cancellation
stories.summary: The Bayreuth Festival had canceled a planned memorial lecture featuring Jewish author and broadcaster Michel Friedman, drawing sharp criticism.

stories.title: World Cup: 'Pride Match' sparks objections in Egypt and Iran
stories.summary: Friday night's game in Seattle was designated as a "Pride Match" well ahead of the World Cup draw. The fixture list then threw up Egypt vs. Iran, two countries in which homosexuality is criminalized.

stories.title: German lawyers: Ban on far-right AfD 'likely successful'
stories.summary: A new report by legal experts has found that efforts to ban Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) could be successful — but there would be political dangers to such a move.

stories.title: World Cup: Morocco's Ismael Saibari set for Bayern Munich
stories.summary: Three nationalities, five languages and soon to be resident in a fourth country: Ismael Saibari, the star of Morocco's World Cup so far, is set to join Bayern Munich. But he has business to finish with the Atlas Lions.

stories.title: Fact check: Throwing cold water on heat wave disinformation
stories.summary: An outdated air-conditioning law, a viral health myth about sleeping with the fan on and the age-old climate change hoax allegation: DW checked what's spreading in Europe's heat wave.

stories.title: Creative climate messaging and other eco wins of the week
stories.summary: An artist in Mali and climate scientists in the US are finding new ways to inform the public about the climate crisis.

stories.title: US, Lebanon, Israel sign framework agreement
stories.summary: US Secretary ‌of ⁠State ⁠Marco ​Rubio has announced ​a trilateral ​framework ‌agreement. Lebanese officials began direct talks with Israel in Washington in April.

stories.title: Trump foe John Bolton pleads guilty in classified docs case
stories.summary: Bolton faces as many as five years in prison. He had earlier served as Trump's national security adviser during the president's first term in office, but later resigned over his differences with Trump.

stories.title: Death toll from Venezuela earthquakes rises to 589, search and rescue efforts continue
stories.summary: The two quakes struck in quick succession, just minutes apart, according to the US Geological Survey, marking it one of the South American nation’s worst disasters in over a century.

stories.title: Gulf states weigh living side by side with Iran as Tehran weaponises Hormuz
stories.summary: As the frontline countries paying the largest price for the Iran war, pressed by severe economic urgencies, the Gulf states are beginning their own firm process towards regional stability with rival Iran, while Tehran continues weaponising the Strait of Hormuz to sow division with its neighbours.

stories.title: Japan fans flood Shibuya as World Cup draw with Sweden sets up Brazil clash
stories.summary: Japan reach World Cup last 32 after 1-1 draw with Sweden on 26 June, prompting celebrations across Tokyo. , sparking huge celebrations in Tokyo’s Shibuya. They now face Brazil in the knockout stage.

stories.title: Historic joy in Abidjan as Ivory Coast reach World Cup knockouts for first time
stories.summary: Ivory Coast reaches World Cup knockout stage for the first time after Nicolas Pépé’s brace beats Curaçao 2-0, sparking huge celebrations in Abidjan.

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes: bodies recovered as rescue efforts continue in La Guaira
stories.summary: Rescue teams worked through the ruins of collapsed buildings in La Guaira on 25 June after twin earthquakes devastated northern Venezuela, killing at least 188 people and injuring around 1,500.

stories.title: Iraq: Ashoura in Karbala draws thousands of Shiite pilgrims for rituals
stories.summary: Thousands of Shiite pilgrims mark Ashoura in Karbala, mourning Imam Hussein’s 680 AD death with processions, prayers and reinforced security.

stories.title: The abundant but expensive energy source that's under your feet
stories.summary: Start-ups are taking fresh approaches to geothermal energy, but will the economics work?

stories.title: Lebanon: thousands mark Ashoura in Nabatieh amid rubble left by Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Thousands of Shiite Muslims marked Ashoura in Nabatieh on 26 June, holding processions through neighbourhoods devastated by recent Israeli airstrikes in a powerful display of resilience.

stories.title: 'Help, we are trapped': Venezuela earthquake victims turn to Euronews' TikTok to beg for rescue"
stories.summary: Lack of resources and patchy telecoms have driven many Venezuelans to despair. In this context, outlets’ social networks, including Euronews, have become key platforms for appeals for help.

stories.title: DR Congo takes Rwanda to international court over decades of conflict
stories.summary: Kinshasa accuses its neighbour of committing various violations since the 1994 Rwanda genocide.

stories.title: Israel declassifies Entebbe raid documents on eve of 50th anniversary
stories.summary: Secret documents reveal Israeli officials privately weighed negotiations before ordering the daring 1976 Uganda hostage rescue operation.

stories.title: Mother dies saving daughter in Venezuela earthquakes
stories.summary: Héctor Bello wrote on social media that "you gave your own life for our daughter" in the quakes which killed at least 920 people.

stories.title: Marco Rubio announces Israel-Lebanon framework deal, hailing it as first step toward peace
stories.summary: The officials did not disclose details of the deal, which was signed by Yechiel Leiter and Nada Hamadeh, Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors to the United States.

stories.title: IRGC denies direct hotline with US, as Trump warns of ceasefire violations in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The spokesperson for the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) denied claims by American officials regarding the establishment of a direct hotline between Iran and the United States concerning the Strait of Hormuz, calling it a "sheer lie".

stories.title: Frozen feasts and cool pools help Rome zoo animals survive heatwave
stories.summary: As a severe heatwave grips Italy, Rome's Bioparco is helping its animals stay cool with frozen treats, chilled pools and climate-controlled shelters while the capital remains under a red heat alert.

stories.title: Watch: BBC reports from La Guaira, one of Venezuela's worst-hit areas
stories.summary: Vanessa Silva visited the coastal state, where rescuers are still working to find survivors under the rubble after twin earthquakes rocked the country.

stories.title: Meloni and Trump: A very public fall-out that is proving very hard to fix
stories.summary: Italy's PM was not long ago being called the "Trump whisperer", but their relationship has gone from public attacks to personal insults.

stories.title: They quit the West for Russia's traditional values, but it wasn't what they expected
stories.summary: The BBC asks Westerners who moved to Russia if life there lives up to expectations.

stories.title: Could you handle a 20-plus hour flight? This airline is banking on it
stories.summary: Some analysts say the first non-stop London to Sydney flight is a major milestone - but would you buy a ticket?

stories.title: Hit South African show gets the world talking about polygamy and cheating
stories.summary: The Zulu-language series about a man with multiple wives is a worldwide hit.

stories.title: Religion row as Texas makes Bible stories required reading in schools
stories.summary: Critics say the new reading requirements infringe on religious freedoms and blur the separation of church and state.

stories.title: Just how much trouble is Canada's economy in?
stories.summary: Five charts help illustrate the state of Canada's economy - and how it's performing compared to other wealthy nations.

stories.title: Reform UK plan to target EU nationals based in Britain ‘absolutely outrageous’
stories.summary: Exclusive: Rights group says Nigel Farage’s party is reneging on promises made during the Brexit referendum campaign UK politics live – latest updates EU nationals based permanently in the UK have expressed alarm over a Reform UK plan to target their rights to accommodation and employment, saying the policy is a betrayal of promises made in the Brexit referendum 10 years ago. Under updated migration policies, Nigel Farage’s party would evict all overseas nationals from social housing and make it

stories.title: Xi Jinping has hosted more than a dozen leaders this year, as ‘middle powers’ look beyond the US
stories.summary: China’s leader wants to promote his alternative to the current world order, and his efforts are being assisted by a capricious US Xi Jinping meets Bangladesh’s new prime minister on Friday, the latest in a wave of world leaders to visit Beijing this year as the Chinese leader builds his influence and economic ties, and seeks to “shift the balance of power” away from the west. Xi’s meeting with Tarique Rahman comes less than two weeks after the Chinese leader welcomed Myanmar’s military chief-tur

stories.title: Fact check: No, Zelenskyy wasn't killed in a Russian airstrike
stories.summary: Amid a flare-up in violence between Russia and Ukraine, viral posts claim Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has died. DW Fact check takes a closer look.

stories.title: Two years after Gen Z uprising, Kenyan protesters return to Nairobi streets
stories.summary: Two years after Kenya's 2024 Gen Z tax revolt shook the nation, protesters gathered in Nairobi on 25 June to seek justice for victims and press for greater government accountability.

stories.title: Cargo ship hit by projectile in Hormuz as Iran issues renewed passage warning
stories.summary: The British military said a cargo ship on the new Omani UN-backed route sustained bridge damage from a projectile, hours after the IRGC warned that vessels without Iranian permission were transiting "illegally".

stories.title: Britain’s six prime ministers since 2016 – ranked!
stories.summary: From Cameron’s Brexit exit to Starmer’s Burnham bow-out, half a dozen PMs have gone. So who’s the best of the bunch? The UK has had six prime ministers in the last 10 years – with a seventh likely to be in place by as early as mid-July. John Crace ranks those who have been booted out of Downing Street between 2016 and 2026. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Constitutional coup’ claims as Zimbabwe senate approves extending presidential term
stories.summary: Opposition figures fear changes will further tighten 83-year-old president Emmerson Mnangagwa’s hold on power Zimbabwe is on the brink of amending its constitution to give the president more time in office, a change that the government says will bring stability but that opponents have labelled a “constitutional coup”. The upper house of Zimbabwe’s parliament voted on Wednesday 75-4 in favour of the constitutional amendments, which will allow President Emmerson Mnangagwa to stay in office until 2

stories.title: Why Germans don't have air conditioning
stories.summary: Unlike the US or parts of Asia, many homes in Germany and northern Europe aren't equipped to deal with extreme heat. But as the world gets hotter, trends are changing.

stories.title: How countries live with earthquakes and build resilience
stories.summary: Earthquakes are a fact of nature, but living with earthquakes doesn't have to mean living in fear. DW takes a closer look at some countries showing how preparation, innovation and determination can save lives.

stories.title: Human rights expert Philippe Sands wins German Peace Prize
stories.summary: From advocating for the law of ecocide to representing Palestinian rights, the descendant of Holocaust survivors tirelessly fights against genocide and crimes against humanity.

stories.title: Germany: Racism is almost a daily reality for Muslims
stories.summary: Insults, discrimination, violence, deaths: Experts warn of growing anti-Muslim racism in Germany — and criticize policymakers.

stories.title: The 'Flamingo Revolution': What's behind Albania's protests?
stories.summary: For weeks now, images of crowds of Albanians protesting on the streets have been relayed around the world. The protesters say they are fighting for democracy. PM Edi Rama insists the movement is part of a hybrid war.

stories.title: Banning social media for teens: Solution or overreaction?
stories.summary: One in seven teens in the European Union spends more than eight hours a day in front of screens, a new study shows. Are social media bans the answer?

stories.title: Germany considers 'opt-out' organ donor option
stories.summary: Germany is one of few European countries that still has an exclusively "opt-in" choice for organ donation — despite long waiting lists for organs. Now, parliament is debating a change.

stories.title: Young people avoiding the news, Reuters report
stories.summary: An alarming number of young people are turning away from the news, according to the Reuters Digital News Report presented at DW's Global Media Forum. Despite this, the report's author says, "journalism still matters."

stories.title: Germany disappoints in loss to Ecuador at 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: Germany had already qualified for the knockouts of the 2026 World Cup, but their performance was still disappointing in a 2-1 defeat.

stories.title: King Charles won't live in Buckingham Palace, reveals taxes
stories.summary: Buckingham Palace has been the British monarch's official residence in London for nearly two centuries. But royal officials say King Charles and Queen Camilla won't reside there.

stories.title: Mumbai monsoon floods streets and disrupts travel
stories.summary: Heavy monsoon rains flood Mumbai, submerging roads, disrupting traffic and forcing residents to wade through waterlogged streets in India’s financial hub.
